commit.message:
Fix refCount race condition in config pipeline (#684)

Fixes the refCount race condition in `restoreForAgent` where two agents
exiting concurrently can decrement `refCount` twice before either checks
`<= 0`, causing `restoreSnapshot` to be called twice and potentially
corrupting configuration state.

## Changes Made

- **Atomic decrement-and-check in `restoreForAgent`**: Moved
`snapshots.delete(absPath)` before `restoreSnapshot()` so that once
refCount hits zero, the snapshot is immediately removed from the map,
preventing any concurrent caller from finding and double-restoring it.
- **Early agent tracking removal**: Moved `agentFiles.delete(agentId)`
before the file iteration loop so that a second concurrent call with the
same agentId returns early as a no-op.
- **New tests**: Added two test cases covering the double-call scenario
(same agent calling `restoreForAgent` twice) and concurrent agent
cleanup not double-restoring the same file.

## Testing

- ✅ All 28 config-pipeline tests pass
- ✅ CodeQL security scan reports no alerts

commit.message:
Fix unnecessary store instances in notificationStore (#682)

Fixes a memory leak where `isAgentVisible()` in `notificationStore.ts`
called `getProjectHubStore(projectId)`, which creates a new hub store if
one doesn't exist. Every notification check for a new project would
create and cache an unnecessary hub store instance.

## Changes Made

- **`src/renderer/plugins/builtin/hub/main.ts`**: Added
`hasProjectHubStore(projectId)` — a read-only lookup that checks if a
hub store already exists in the `projectHubStores` map without creating
one.
- **`src/renderer/stores/notificationStore.ts`**: Guarded the
`getProjectHubStore()` call in `isAgentVisible()` with
`hasProjectHubStore()`, so the store is only accessed if it already
exists. If no store exists for that project, the hub pane visibility
check is skipped (since no hub store means the agent can't be visible in
a hub pane).
- **`src/renderer/plugins/builtin/hub/main.test.ts`**: Added 4 tests for
`hasProjectHubStore()` covering: export exists, returns false for
unknown projects, returns false for null, and returns true after store
creation.

## Testing

- All 39 tests pass (35 existing + 4 new)
- Code review: clean
- CodeQL security scan: no alerts

Add request body size limit to hook server (#681)

Adds a 1MB request body size limit to the hook server to prevent memory
exhaustion from arbitrarily large requests.

## Changes Made

- **Body size limit**: Added a `MAX_BODY_SIZE` constant (1MB) in
`src/main/services/hook-server.ts`
- **Size enforcement**: The `data` event handler now tracks accumulated
body size and responds with HTTP 413 (Payload Too Large) when exceeded,
then destroys the request stream
- **Guard against multiple events**: Added an early return in the `data`
handler to prevent multiple response writes if additional chunks arrive
before the stream is fully destroyed
- **Tests**: Added 2 tests in `src/main/services/hook-server.test.ts`
verifying that oversized requests are rejected with 413 and normal-sized
requests continue to work

## Testing

- ✅ All 30 tests pass (28 existing + 2 new)
- ✅ No CodeQL security alerts

commit.message:
fix: remove ZEN2/ZEN3/SKYLAKE from OpenBLAS DYNAMIC_LIST

OpenBLAS 0.3.31 compiles generic C dot/nrm2 kernels for ZEN2, ZEN3 and
SKYLAKE using AVX2+FMA intrinsics but omits -mfma from the compiler
invocation when DYNAMIC_LIST is set, causing an always_inline target
mismatch build error on all platforms.

Fix: remove the three offending targets:
- Generic list: NEHALEM SANDYBRIDGE HASWELL SKYLAKEX ZEN (was +ZEN2 ZEN3)
- AVX2 list:    HASWELL SKYLAKEX (was +SKYLAKE ZEN2 ZEN3)

commit.message:
fix: rename Alumni.svg to alumni.svg and deploy testing on merge (#93)

## Problem

Two issues were causing the mobile app to misbehave on the testing
environment:

### 1. Missing `alumni.svg` (case sensitivity)
The asset file was `assets/images/Alumni.svg` (capital A) on macOS
(case-insensitive filesystem), but the Flutter-generated code and nginx
on the Linux Docker container expected `assets/images/alumni.svg`
(lowercase). This caused a 404 on every page that renders the alumni
logo.

### 2. Testing environment not updated on PR merge
The `deploy-testing` job only ran on pull request events, not on push to
`main`. So when PR #91 merged, only **production** was updated — the
testing environment kept running the old code.

## Changes
- `assets/images/Alumni.svg` → `assets/images/alumni.svg` (rename, Linux
case fix)
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — `deploy-testing` now also runs on
`push` to `main`

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 9.5: Resource Management API

Implement public API for font and image loading with automatic
resource cleanup (ROADMAP.md Phase 9.5).

## Implementation

### ResourceManager (resource.go - 304 LOC)
- Centralized management of fonts and images
- Default embedded font (SDF atlas, ASCII 0x20-0x7E)
- PNG/JPEG image loading via stdlib decoders
- Automatic cleanup on app shutdown

### Public API
- App.DefaultFont() - returns embedded SDF font
- App.LoadFont(path, size) - loads font at specified size
- App.LoadImage(path) - loads PNG/JPEG from file
- Font.Size() - returns font size in points
- Image.Size() - returns image dimensions

### Integration
- ResourceManager initialized in App.initRenderer()
- Cleanup integrated into App.cleanup()
- Resources field added to App struct

## Testing (resource_test.go - 272 LOC)
- 13 comprehensive tests, all passing
- Font loading with multiple sizes
- Image loading from file and reader
- Cleanup verification
- Image format conversion (imageToRGBA)

## Demo (cmd/resource-demo/)
- Demonstrates font and image loading APIs
- Creates test images in memory
- Validates public API usability

## Quality
- Zero regressions in existing tests
- All 59 test packages passing
- Race detector clean
- go vet clean (known false positive excluded)
- New functions ≤10 cyclomatic complexity

## Deferred

commit.message:
Remove redundant abortUpload mutation

The tus-server gem already provides DELETE /files/:uid for aborting uploads.
The abortUpload GraphQL mutation was just a thin wrapper with no added
business logic, so it's redundant.

Keep only:
- finalizeUpload: Essential GraphQL mutation that moves files from
  tmp/tus_uploads to the final destination (Config::Video.settings_upload_path)

Clients now use:
- DELETE /files/:uid (tus REST API) to abort uploads
- finalizeUpload(uploadId) (GraphQL) to promote to production

All tests pass: 34 runs, 65 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors

commit.message:
Replace custom chunked upload system with tus.io protocol

Implements RFC-like tus resumable upload protocol for large file transfers.
Benefits over custom system:
- Standard protocol with 6 official client libraries (JS, Python, Java, Go, Android, Swift)
- Resumable uploads after network failures
- Built-in checksum validation
- Automatic expiration of incomplete uploads (48 hours)
- Works like FTP: chunk-based, atomic, multipart
- Proven at Vimeo and Google scale

Changes:
- Add tus-server gem (~> 2.0)
- Mount tus-server at /files endpoint (REST API)
- Rewrite FinalizeUpload & AbortUpload mutations for tus
- Remove custom InitiateUpload mutation (tus handles via POST /files)
- Remove custom API controller (tus handles chunks via PATCH /files/:uid)
- Remove AssemblyJob (tus handles assembly natively)

API changes:
- Client initiates: POST /files → tus server returns Location header with UID
- Client resumes: HEAD /files/:uid → tus server returns Upload-Offset
- Client sends chunk: PATCH /files/:uid with Content-Range header
- Client finalizes: GraphQL finalizeUpload(uploadId) mutation
- Client aborts: DELETE /files/:uid or GraphQL abortUpload mutation

All tests pass: 36 runs, 72 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors

commit.message:
Reimplement Agenda as a BQL page

SPC a a now opens a page titled 'Agenda' instead of a custom
overlay. If no Agenda page exists, one is created from a built-in
template with three BQL query blocks:
- Overdue: tasks | where not done and due < today
- This Week: tasks | where not done and due this week
- No Due Date: tasks | where not done and due = none

The page is a regular Bloom page — editable, searchable via
SPC f f, customisable. Users can add sections, change queries,
mix with prose. The old agenda overlay code (AgendaState,
handle_agenda_key) remains for now but is no longer the primary
agenda UI.
